name: City tour circular through the emblems of Linares
name_original: City tour circular por los emblemas de Linares
region_intro: Hiking around Linares offers diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrains with panoramic views to lush forests and rivers. The region features varied ecosystems, including ancient forests where native flora thrives. Hikers can explore dramatic rock formations and alpine meadows, alongside water features like the Rio Achibueno. This area provides a comprehensive network of trails suitable for various experience levels. Best hiking trails around Linares The most popular hiking route is PR-A 510 Cástulo, Desired City Trail, a 9.2 miles (14.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route allows hikers to explore ancient ruins combined with diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is City tour circular through the emblems of Linares, an easy 2.8 miles (4.6 km) path. This route explores urban landmarks and is suitable for all fitness levels. Local hikers also love the Monument to Andrés Segovia – Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos loop from Linares, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through cultural sites, often completed in about 3 hours 4 minutes. Hiking around Linares is defined by mountainous terrain, rivers, and ancient forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more strenuous ascents. The routes in Linares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 170 hikers have used komoot to explore Linares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Linares?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes around Linares, offering a variety of experiences. These include 10 easy trails, 13 moderate routes, and 1 more challenging option, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Linares?
A: Yes, Linares offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984646193/city-tour-circular-through-the-emblems-of-linares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour circular through the emblems of Linares&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.6 km path that explores urban landmarks. Another easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38140487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos – Doña Luci Gardens loop from Estadio municipal de Linarejos&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 7.5 km long.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Linares?
A: Linares boasts diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrains with panoramic views to lush forests, crystal-clear rivers, and picturesque waterfalls. You can explore dramatic rock formations, alpine meadows, and ancient forests where native flora and fauna thrive. Notable water features include the Rio Achibueno and various waterfalls like those in Parque Nacional Radal Siete Tazas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Linares?
A: Yes, many routes in Linares are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983492254/pr-a-510-castulo-desired-city-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-A 510 Cástulo, Desired City Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.9 km circular route that allows you to explore ancient ruins and diverse landscapes. Another popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to Andrés Segovia – Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos loop from Linares&lt;/a&gt;, covering 11.8 km.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the hiking trails in Linares?
A: Hiking trails around Linares often lead to fascinating landmarks and natural attractions. You can discover historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3509072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalosa Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to impressive castles such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3144645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Baños de la Encina (Burgalimar Castle)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilches Castle&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4008210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rumblar Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints offering stunning vistas of the reservoir.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Linares?
A: The hiking experience in Linares is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse terrain, which includes mountainous areas, rivers, and ancient forests, providing options for all ability levels. The combination of natural beauty and cultural sites, like those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to Andrés Segovia – Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos loop&lt;/a&gt;, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water elements like rivers or waterfalls?
A: Yes, Linares is rich in water features. The Rio Achibueno is a prominent draw, offering opportunities for hiking alongside its pristine banks and even swimming. The region also features thermal springs at Rio Gloria and notable waterfalls, such as those in Parque Nacional Radal Siete Tazas, known as 'The Seven Cups,' and the Cascadas de Las Animas y Los Quemados.

Q: Can I find trails that combine nature with historical or cultural sites?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983492254/pr-a-510-castulo-desired-city-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-A 510 Cástulo, Desired City Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, allowing hikers to explore the ancient Ibero-Roman city of Cástulo amidst diverse landscapes. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to Andrés Segovia – Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos loop from Linares&lt;/a&gt;, leads through significant cultural sites.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: While most trails are easy to moderate, Linares does offer more strenuous options. The region's mountainous terrain, with routes like the Cervero Peak loop, provides challenging ascents with significant elevation gains, rewarding hikers with extensive vistas. One difficult route is listed among the 24 available trails, catering to those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: What is the longest moderate trail available in Linares?
A: The longest moderate trail listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos – Chimney of the Cruz loop from Antigua Estación de Madrid&lt;/a&gt;. This route spans approximately 17.4 km and typically takes around 4 hours 30 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming after a hike in Linares?
A: Yes, the Rio Achibueno is noted for its pristine condition and offers opportunities for swimming in its crystal-clear waters. It's a popular spot for hikers looking to cool off after exploring the surrounding trails.

Q: What kind of wildlife and plant life can I expect to see?
A: Linares features diverse ecosystems, including ancient forests where native flora and fauna thrive. Areas like Reserva Nacional Altos de Lircay are recognized for their pristine wilderness, offering a chance to observe local wildlife and a variety of plant species.
tips:
The Linares Bullring, also known as the Santa Margarita Bullring, was built in 1866, and inaugurated a year later with a Miura bullfight, with the presence of the swordsmen Antonio Carmona, "El Gordito" and Manuel Carmona , "Baker".

   The most well-known and outstanding event that occurred in this arena is the death of the bullfighter Manolete in August 1947, gored by the bull Islero. Next to the gang gate there is a ceramic and marble plaque commemorating that tragic afternoon at the fair.

   It has a ring 50 meters in diameter and its alley 1.30 meters wide. It has a capacity of 10,500 spectators.

Paseo de Linarejos is one of the most important streets in Linares. It is 500 meters long and 30 meters wide. It consists of a central street and two narrower side streets. It shows a varied vegetation in its gardens. The lampposts are cast iron in the modernist style, made in the San Antonio de Sevilla foundry in 1881.

   Tiled benches display old photographs of the city. At its northern end is the Glorieta de América, where the Monument to Andrés Segovia stands.

This is a monument in homage to the figure of the international guitarist Andrés Segovia. It is the work of the sculptor Julio López Hernández made in bronze. The monument is located on a plinth, inside the central fountain of the Glorieta de América, at the end of Paseo Virgen de Linarejos.
